description:
The journald system may store log files in volatile memory or locally on disk.
If the logs are only stored in volatile memory they will be lost upon reboot.
rationale: Log files contain valuable data and need to be persistent to aid in possible investigations.
ocil_clause: is commented out or not configured correctly
ocil:
Storing logs with persistent storage ensures they are available after a reboot or system crash. Run the command below to verify that logs are being persistently stored to disk. 
grep "^\sStorage" /etc/systemd/journald.conf /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf
and it should return 
Storage=persistent
title: Ensure journald is configured to write log files to persistent disk
